summary: Joel Spolsky briefly recommends Steve Krug's new book 'Rocket Surgery Made Easy,' a follow-up to the usability classic 'Don't Make Me Think.' He describes it as a concise and fun guide to running simple hallway usability tests. The post serves as a short book endorsement rather than a detailed review. Spolsky highlights the book's practical focus on improving software and website usability through lightweight testing methods.
key_points: Steve Krug's 'Rocket Surgery Made Easy' is a sequel to the influential 'Don't Make Me Think'
The book focuses on running simple 'hallway' usability tests as a practical approach to improving usability
Spolsky endorses the book as concise, fun, and highly recommended
money_quotes: Rocket Surgery Made Easy is a terrific, short, concise, fun guide to running simple "hallway" usability tests to improve the usability of your software and websites.
key_insight: Simple hallway usability testing, as outlined in Steve Krug's latest book, remains one of the most practical approaches to improving software and website usability.
